title: Huernia andreaeana
taxonomy: Huernia andreaeana is classified within the family Apocynaceae and is further categorized as a species of the genus Huernia. This genus is a part of the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. More specifically, Huernia andreaeana is a species of the Asclepiadaceae subfamily, which is a type of flowering plant. The classification of Huernia andreaeana is also recognized in the APG IV system. Additionally, it belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta.

intro: Huernia andreaeana, also known as the starfish Huernia, is a species of succulent plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to southern Africa. Traditionally, Huernia andreaeana has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Huernia andreaeana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Huernia andreaeana is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of tinctures and ointments to harness its therapeutic benefits.
